narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this feels deeply personal, and I'm here to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam the way it did, based on the story your profile tells. At the top of what shaped this 42% chance of not passing the bar was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, the strongest signal pulling toward that outcome, suggesting challenges in sustaining strong performance as things got tougher. Right alongside that, your LSAT score of 22.0 stood out as a major factor tipping the scales toward failure, as it reflects the kind of early benchmark that often forecasts bar struggles for students like you. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 reinforced this pattern, painting a picture of consistently lower rankings in a competitive school environment from the start. Layered on top was your low family income, which the model saw as adding real hurdles, like fewer resources for the intense prep many need. Your Asian background and female gender each nudged the prediction further toward this risk, reflecting patterns we've seen in past students' journeys. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.7 and full-time study commitment couldn't fully counterbalance these, though they were the gentler influences here. Together, these pieces wove a narrative of resilience tested early and often, leading the model to predict you'd face steep odds on the bar. This isn't about your potential—it's a data-driven snapshot of similar paths that hit roadblocks. Many students in your shoes have pivoted successfully with targeted steps, like LSAT retakes or extra academic support. You've got a 3.7 UGPA showing real grit; lean into that as you chart your next move. We're rooting for you to rewrite this story.
